{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also! == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)}}

To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|12=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|12=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)}}

It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|12=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|12=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|12=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|12=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)}}

It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|12=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|12=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|12=draft|12=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=draft|12=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=draft|12=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|12=draft|12=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=draft|12=draft}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft|12=draft}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|12=draft|12=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|12=draft|12=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=draft|12=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=draft|12=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|12=draft|12=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)}}

revision.comment: 48 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鷲神社 (台東区) up to rev 103731869
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鷲神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
叉木と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]三丁目18番7号 |ISO = |緯度度 = 35|緯度分=43|緯度秒=21.2 |経度度 = 139|経度分=47|経度秒=30.7 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]]
[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |神殿 = |別名 = おとりさま |札所等 = [[浅草名所七福神]]、[[下町八福神]] |例祭 = 11月酉の日（酉の市） |神事 = |アクセス = }} '''鷲神社'''（おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]3丁目にある[[神社]]である。「おとりさま」の通称でも呼ばれ、11月の例祭は「[[酉の市]]（とりのいち）」として広く知られる。 ==祭神== *[[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]] *[[日本武尊]] ==歴史== 言い伝えによれば、古来この地に[[天日鷲神]]が祀られており、その神社に[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]が東征の折に戦勝を祈願したとされるが、実際は隣接する[[長国寺 (台東区)|長国寺]]に祀られていた鷲宮に始まるという。[[江戸時代]]中期から[[酉の市]]で知られ、[[東京都]][[足立区]]花畑7丁目の[[大鷲神社 (足立区)|大鷲神社]]の「おおとり」に対し、鷲神社は「しんとり」と称された。[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]に伴い、長国寺から独立し鷲神社となった。 ==所在地== *東京都台東区千束三丁目18番7号 == アクセス == === 鉄道 === * [[東京地下鉄|東京メトロ]][[東京メトロ日比谷線|日比谷線]] [[入谷駅 (東京都)|入谷駅]]又は[[三ノ輪駅]]から徒歩10分程度 * [[首都圏新都市鉄道つくばエクスプレス|つくばエクスプレス]] [[浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|浅草駅]]から徒歩10分程度 === バス === * [[都バス]][[都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|都08]]千束 * [[めぐりん (台東区)|台東区循環バスめぐりん]]「北めぐりん」30番 台東病院バス停から徒歩3分程度　 なお、めぐりんは酉の市の期間中は交通規制により台東病院停留所を迂回するため28番の千束二丁目が最寄りとなる。 == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|叉木と大熊手（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|叉木（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|神楽殿と社殿（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|社殿（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|手水舎（2010年1月撮影） }} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat}} *[[酉の市]] *[[長国寺 (台東区)|長国寺]] *[[レッド・エンタテインメント]] - [[ゲーム]]・[[アニメーション]]等各種コンテンツの企画・製作会社。前身となるレッドカンパニーは鷲神社に隣接する同神社が所有していた敷地内に社屋を構えていた{{Cite web|和書|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=聖地巡礼・ゲーム編 浅草紀行|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}}。 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://otorisama.or.jp/ 鷲神社 公式サイト] *[http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ 浅草 鷲神社 酉の市] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとりしんしや たいとうく}} [[Category:台東区の神社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]]

叉木と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]三丁目18番7号 |ISO = |緯度度 = 35|緯度分=43|緯度秒=21.2 |経度度 = 139|経度分=47|経度秒=30.7 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]]
[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |神殿 = |別名 = おとりさま |札所等 = [[浅草名所七福神]]、[[下町八福神]] |例祭 = 11月酉の日（酉の市） |神事 = |アクセス = }} '''鷲神社'''（おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]3丁目にある[[神社]]である。「おとりさま」の通称でも呼ばれ、11月の例祭は「[[酉の市]]（とりのいち）」として広く知られる。 ==祭神== *[[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]] *[[日本武尊]] ==歴史== 言い伝えによれば、古来この地に[[天日鷲神]]が祀られており、その神社に[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]が東征の折に戦勝を祈願したとされるが、実際は隣接する[[長国寺 (台東区)|長国寺]]に祀られていた鷲宮に始まるという。[[江戸時代]]中期から[[酉の市]]で知られ、[[東京都]][[足立区]]花畑7丁目の[[大鷲神社 (足立区)|大鷲神社]]の「おおとり」に対し、鷲神社は「しんとり」と称された。[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]に伴い、長国寺から独立し鷲神社となった。 ==所在地== *東京都台東区千束三丁目18番7号 == アクセス == === 鉄道 === * [[東京地下鉄|東京メトロ]][[東京メトロ日比谷線|日比谷線]] [[入谷駅 (東京都)|入谷駅]]又は[[三ノ輪駅]]から徒歩10分程度 * [[首都圏新都市鉄道つくばエクスプレス|つくばエクスプレス]] [[浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|浅草駅]]から徒歩10分程度 === バス === * [[都バス]][[都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|都08]]千束 * [[めぐりん (台東区)|台東区循環バスめぐりん]]「北めぐりん」30番 台東病院バス停から徒歩3分程度　 なお、めぐりんは酉の市の期間中は交通規制により台東病院停留所を迂回するため28番の千束二丁目が最寄りとなる。 == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|叉木と大熊手（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|叉木（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|神楽殿と社殿（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|社殿（2010年1月撮影） |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|手水舎（2010年1月撮影） }} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat}} *[[酉の市]] *[[長国寺 (台東区)|長国寺]] *[[レッド・エンタテインメント]] - [[ゲーム]]・[[アニメーション]]等各種コンテンツの企画・製作会社。前身となるレッドカンパニーは鷲神社に隣接する同神社が所有していた敷地内に社屋を構えていた{{Cite web|和書|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=聖地巡礼・ゲーム編 浅草紀行|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}}。 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[https://otorisama.or.jp/ 鷲神社 公式サイト] *[http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ 浅草 鷲神社 酉の市] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとりしんしや たいとうく}} [[Category:台東区の神社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame-No-Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Taitō Ward)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|ja|浅草駅 (首都圏新都市鉄道)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji (Taitō Ward)|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|ja|レッド・エンタテインメント|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Line|qq=}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk Entertainment|qq=}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|qq=}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] {{ill|Hibiya Line|ja|東京メトロ日比谷線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Line|qq=}} [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Legends of Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]]

It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taitō Ward]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}}

To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}}
