Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja.JPG|250px]]
Crossed Logs and Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|21.2|N|139|47|30.7|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}}

During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|qid=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|qid=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|qid=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|qid=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|qq=|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|qq=|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[sn:Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] [[en:Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}}

{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|qq=|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|qq=|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|qid=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|qid=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11676284}}

[[commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]

{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]] [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|Hibiya Line]] [[:en:Iriya Station (Tokyo)|Iriya Station]] or [[:en:Minowa Station|Minowa Station]], about a 10-minute walk * [[:en:Tsukuba Express|Tsukuba Express]] [[:en:Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)]], about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * [[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]] {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * [[:en:Red Entertainment|Red Entertainment]] - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]

[[co:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Taito)]][[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676284]] Wikidata name: Ōtori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} [[Taitō Ward]] [[Senzoku]] 3-18-7 |ISO = |Latitude Degrees = 35|Latitude Minutes=43|Latitude Seconds=21.2 |Longitude Degrees = 139|Longitude Minutes=47|Longitude Seconds=30.7 |Label Position = |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Sacred Object = |Rank = |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = Otorisama |Pilgrimage Sites = {{ill|Asakusa Nanashifukujin|ja|浅草名所七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Nanashifukujin|1=Asakusa Nanashifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557223}}, {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361236}} |festival = November Tori-no-hi (Tori-no-Ichi) |Rituals = |Access = |location = {{ill|Senzoku|WD=Q11405606}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72256806|N|139.79188611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Senzoku]], [[Taitō Ward]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. It is also known by the nickname "Otorisama," and its annual November festival is widely recognized as {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Ame no Wakahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} == History == According to legend, the shrine originally enshrined [[Otori-sama]] at this location. During his eastern expedition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is said to have prayed for victory at this shrine. However, historical records suggest the shrine began as part of {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}}, where a shrine to Ōtori was located. The shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms of the early Meiji period and was officially named Ōtori Shrine. Since the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, Ōtori Shrine has been associated with the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival. To distinguish it from {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|qq=}} in Adachi Ward, which is referred to as "Ōtori," this shrine has been nicknamed "Shintori." == location == * 3-18-7 Senzoku, Taitō Ward, Tokyo == Access == === Rail === * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|en|Tokyo Metro|WD=Q682894|lt=Tokyo Metro}} {{ill|Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|en|Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|WD=Q1196223|lt=Hibiya Line}} {{ill|Iriya Station (Tokyo)|en|Iriya Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q1073228|lt=Iriya Station}} or {{ill|Minowa Station|en|Minowa Station|WD=Q1072979|lt=Minowa Station}}, about a 10-minute walk * {{ill|Tsukuba Express|en|Tsukuba Express|WD=Q1377699|lt=Tsukuba Express}} {{ill|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|en|Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)|WD=Q4803450|lt=Asakusa Station (Tsukuba Express)}}, about a 10-minute walk === Bus === * {{ill|Toei Bus|en|Toei Bus|WD=Q859227|lt=Toei Bus}} {{ill|Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#都08系統（グリーンリバー）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Bus Minamisenju Depot#To08 Route (Green River)|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}} "Senzoku" stop * {{ill|Megurin (Taitō Ward)|ja|めぐりん (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Megurin (Taitō Ward)|1=Megurin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11279668}} "North Megurin" Line, Stop 30: Taitō Hospital, about a 3-minute walk During the Tori-no-Ichi festival, the Megurin bus bypasses Taitō Hospital due to traffic restrictions, making Stop 28: Senzoku 2-Chome the nearest stop. == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Ootori jinja1.jpg|Crossed Logs and Large Decorative Rake (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja2.jpg|Crossed Logs (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja3.jpg|Kagura Hall and Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja4.jpg|Main Hall (January 2010) |File:Ootori jinja5.jpg|Chōzuya (January 2010) }} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōkoku-ji|ja|長国寺_(台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkoku-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Red Entertainment|en|Red Entertainment|WD=Q564275|lt=Red Entertainment}} - A company involved in game and animation production. Its predecessor, Red Company, was headquartered on land owned by Ōtori Shrine.{{Cite web|ja|url=https://game-creators.jp/media/column/131/|title=Holy Pilgrimage - Games Edition: Asakusa Chronicles|publisher=GAME CREATORS|date=2019-10-04|accessdate=2020-01-17}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] * [http://www.asakusa-torinoichi.tokyo/ Asakusa Ōtori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|version=103731869|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鷲神社 (台東区)]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Taito)}} {{wikidata link|Q11676284}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]
